sources.exact_document_title: Selected practice recommendations for contraceptive use, fourth edition
sources.population: People seeking initiation, continuation, switching, or support for contraceptive method use after medical eligibility is established.
sources.applicability_note: Global exact guidance for informed contraceptive choice and practical method use; partial for UAE-specific law, prescribing, and full medical-eligibility assessment, which requires the companion MEC and local review. This batch also reviews method-follow-up, entered effects, and emergency-contraception sections. Eligibility, product selection, dosing, procedures and UAE availability remain clinician responsibilities. exact_sections.heading: 1.1 Purpose; 1.2 Scope; 1.3 Target audience
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines the guideline as practical contraceptive-use guidance for providers once medical eligibility has been established.

exact_sections.heading: 1.5 Contraceptive choice and informed consent
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Requires understandable information, voluntary informed choice, client preference, consent, and freedom from coercion.

exact_sections.heading: 1.6 Quality of care and access to products
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies method information on effectiveness, correct use, common effects, risks and benefits, return-to-clinic signs, fertility, STI protection, and opportunities for questions.

exact_sections.heading: 3.1 Examinations and tests; 3.2 Contraceptive eligibility; 5.1 Reasonable certainty that a woman is not pregnant
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines method-specific examination and test classification, preserves medical eligibility as a separate assessment, and supports pregnancy-status review before method initiation.

exact_sections.heading: Method-specific continuation, common effects and return guidance
exact_sections.locator: fourth edition method chapters and recommendations
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting the method actually used, adherence or continuation, entered bleeding or other effects, satisfaction and clinician-arranged review. It does not diagnose an adverse reaction or select a replacement method.

exact_sections.heading: Emergency contraceptive pills and copper-bearing intrauterine devices for emergency contraception
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting intercourse timing, method discussion, pregnancy possibility and clinician-selected emergency-contraception care with method and eligibility qualifiers retained; it does not autonomously select or provide a method.

exact_sections.heading: Contraceptive implants — initiation, continuation and follow-up
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting an identified implant, insertion date or site, bleeding or other entered concerns, satisfaction and clinician-arranged review with pregnancy and eligibility qualifiers retained.

exact_sections.heading: Copper-bearing and levonorgestrel-releasing intrauterine devices — initiation and follow-up
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting an identified device, insertion details, strings or examination actually assessed, bleeding or pain, pregnancy and infection context, and clinician-arranged follow-up without asserting correct position or selecting removal.
